vocab.meaning: a person you like and know well
to put food in your mouth

grammar.body: Use am with I, is with he/she/it, are with you/we/they.
grammar.eg: I am Mia. She is my friend. They are here.

reading.passage: Hi! My name is Mia. This is my house. I live here with my best friend, Sam. We eat dinner together and drink water with our food.

q.q: What do they drink with food?

lines.t: Yes, I am. Are you Mia's friend?
Yes, I am. Nice to meet you!

retell.prompt: Retell Mia's introduction in your own words.

writing.tip: A simple introduction: say your name, then one fact about you.

task.prompt: Write a 2-sentence introduction about yourself. Use the word "name".

vocab.meaning: the early part of the day
a job; to do a job
vocab.ex: She goes to work by bus.

vocab.meaning: coins and notes we pay with

grammar.body: Use a/an for one general thing (an before a vowel sound), the for a specific one.
grammar.eg: a house · an apple · the house on my street

reading.passage: Every morning, Tom goes to work. He buys food for lunch. He does not spend much money. He always has time for a coffee.

q.q: When does Tom go to work?
Does he spend a lot of money?

lines.t: It's nine o'clock. Time for work!
Do you have time for breakfast?

shadow: I go to work every morning.

retell.prompt: Retell Tom's morning in your own words.

writing.tip: Use a/an for one general thing, the for a specific one.

task.prompt: Write 2 sentences about your morning routine. Use the word "morning".

vocab.meaning: a large vehicle that carries many people
vocab.ex: I take the bus to school.

vocab.meaning: a paper that lets you travel
a road in a town with houses

vocab.ex: The shop is near my house.

grammar.body: Use there is for one thing, there are for more than one.
grammar.eg: There is a bus stop. There are two shops.

reading.passage: The bus stop is near my house. I buy a ticket and wait on the street. To find it, turn left at the bank.

lines.t: Excuse me, is there a bus stop near here?
Yes, there is. Turn left at the bank.

q.q: Is there a bus stop near?

shadow: Is there a bus stop near here?

retell.prompt: Explain how to find the bus stop, in your own words.

writing.tip: Giving directions: use turn left/right and near / next to.

gap.q: I buy a ___ for the bus.

task.prompt: Write directions from your home to a shop (2 sentences). Use "near".
